description: Remove agent setup friction in the CLI and SDK so a fresh agent can go from zero to a completed capped purchase with the fewest commands, no silent waits, and no OTP loops.

userStories.title: Emit immediate startup JSON event in CLI
userStories.description: As a calling agent, I want the CLI to emit a JSON startup event immediately so I can distinguish npx/package startup from a hung payment.
acceptanceCriteria: When --json is passed, main() in packages/cli/src/index.ts writes {"type":"startup","message":"loading Rubicon CLI"} to stdout before reading config or constructing the client
The startup event is emitted for buy, quickstart-read, doctor, and read commands
No startup event is emitted without --json
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Fail fast on unsupported Node versions
userStories.description: As an agent on an older Node runtime, I want a clear error instead of a cryptic SyntaxError from the JSON import attribute so I can fix my environment quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/cli/package.json declares an engines field with the minimum supported Node version

The JSON import attribute in packages/cli/src/index.ts (import packageJson ... with { type: "json" }) is replaced with a mechanism that works on Node 18+ (e.g. createRequire or readFile), or a top-of-file runtime version check prints a clear error naming the required Node version
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Make --first optional for rubicon buy
userStories.description: As a calling agent, I want rubicon buy --goal ... --max-usdc ... to work without the ceremonial --first flag so the primary command has no avoidable failure mode.
acceptanceCriteria: rubicon buy --goal "x" --max-usdc 0.10 --json succeeds without --first (first-article selection remains the default behavior)
--first is still accepted and behaves identically
The MISSING_FIRST error in packages/cli/src/quickstart.ts is removed
Help text in showHelp() shows --first as optional or omits it
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Pass --testnet to Circle auth status checks for testnet chains
userStories.description: As an agent buying an ARC-TESTNET article, I want the CLI to check the testnet Circle session (not the mainnet one) so a successful testnet login is not misreported as NOT_LOGGED_IN.
acceptanceCriteria: circleAuthStatus in packages/cli/src/circle.ts accepts a testnet/environment option and appends --testnet to the circle wallet status arguments when set
runBuy and runDoctor in packages/cli/src/quickstart.ts pass the testnet option when the resolved chain/environment is testnet (ARC-TESTNET)
A unit test asserts the circle wallet status invocation includes --testnet when chain is ARC-TESTNET and omits it for mainnet
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the root cause of the OTP loop the hosted skill.md spends ~20 lines teaching agents to work around.

userStories.title: Auto-fallback to npx when Circle CLI is not on PATH (CLI package)
userStories.description: As an agent in a fresh npx context without Circle CLI installed, I want Rubicon to invoke Circle via npx automatically so setup does not dead-end on missing_cli.
acceptanceCriteria: runCircleCli in packages/cli/src/circle.ts, on ENOENT for both circle and circle-cli, retries via npx -y --package @circle-fin/cli circle <args> before classifying the error
The npx fallback is skippable via an env var (e.g. RUBICON_NO_NPX_FALLBACK=1) and respects an existing CIRCLE_CLI_COMMAND override
missing_cli guidance text mentions the npx form npx -y --package @circle-fin/cli circle ... instead of only 'Install Circle CLI'
A unit test with an injected runner asserts the npx fallback arguments
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Windows-safe Circle CLI invocation
userStories.description: As a Windows agent, I want Circle CLI calls to resolve npm's circle.cmd shim so payments work without POSIX assumptions.
acceptanceCriteria: runCircleCli in packages/cli/src/circle.ts and runCircleCli in packages/agent-sdk/src/circle-cli-gateway-payment.ts resolve the circle.cmd/.exe shim on win32 (e.g. execFile with shell:true on win32, or explicit .cmd resolution) instead of assuming execFile("circle") works
Non-Windows behavior is unchanged
A unit test covers the win32 command resolution path (platform injected or mocked)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: skill.md warns third-party wrappers about exactly this bug, but Rubicon's own runners have it.

userStories.title: Add rubicon login command wrapping Circle OTP flow
userStories.description: As a calling agent, I want rubicon login <email> and rubicon login --request <id> --otp <code> so first-time auth is two rubicon commands instead of a separate npx package with four manual steps.
acceptanceCriteria: rubicon login <email> [--testnet] --json initiates Circle OTP login via the Circle CLI runner (with npx fallback) and prints JSON containing the request id
rubicon login --request <id> --otp <code> [--testnet] --json completes login and prints a JSON success/failure result
--testnet defaults to true when the configured/default chain is ARC-TESTNET
The OTP code is never written to config or echoed back in output beyond the success result
Command appears in showHelp() usage
Unit tests with an injected Circle runner cover init and completion argument construction
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Classify Circle Terms errors with a structured code and exact command
userStories.description: As a calling agent, I want terms-acceptance failures to surface as TERMS_NOT_ACCEPTED with the exact command to run so I can recover without parsing free text.
acceptanceCriteria: classifyCircleError in packages/cli/src/circle.ts detects terms/agreement acceptance output and returns a terms_not_accepted code with guidance containing the exact circle terms command to run
runBuy surfaces this as CliError code TERMS_NOT_ACCEPTED with the command in the message
A unit test feeds a terms-required Circle error string and asserts the classification
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: NOT_LOGGED_IN errors name the exact rubicon login recovery command
userStories.description: As a calling agent that hit NOT_LOGGED_IN, I want the JSON error to include the exact next command (rubicon login <email> with the right --testnet flag) so recovery needs no runbook lookup.
acceptanceCriteria: not_logged_in guidance in packages/cli/src/circle.ts references rubicon login <email> [--testnet] instead of generic 'Run Circle CLI login/auth again'
The --json error object for NOT_LOGGED_IN from rubicon buy includes a recovery field or guidance string containing the rubicon login command with --testnet when the article chain is testnet
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Default SDK baseUrl to the hosted gateway
userStories.description: As an SDK user following the README quickstart, I want the client to target the hosted gateway by default so the first snippet works without running a local gateway.
acceptanceCriteria: RubiconClient in packages/agent-sdk/src/agent-client.ts defaults baseUrl to the hosted gateway URL (same value as the CLI's HOSTED_GATEWAY_URL) instead of http://localhost:8787
packages/agent-sdk/README.md quickstart and baseUrl docs reflect the new default and show how to point at a local gateway
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Sync the repo's stale skill.md with the published v2 runbook
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the repo's skill.md to match the published 2.x runbook so agents never read the contradictory v1.1.0 rules (e.g. 'Never accept Circle Terms' vs 'run terms yourself').
acceptanceCriteria: /skill.md at the repo root matches the 2.0.3 content currently published in rubicon-marketing/public/skill.md, updated for changes made in earlier stories (rubicon login, optional --first, startup event now real)
The OTP-loop workaround section is simplified to reflect the --testnet status fix from US-004
Pinned CLI version references use the current packages/cli/package.json version
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Root skill.md rewritten as v2.1.0 (rubicon login recovery, optional --first, real startup event, cli 0.1.8). The rubicon-app and rubicon-marketing copies live in sibling repos and must be synced there after npm publish of cli 0.1.8.
